serviceDescription: Amazon Timestream is a fast, scalable, and serverless time series database service for IoT and operational applications that makes it easy to store and analyze trillions of events per day up to 1,000 times faster and at as little as 1/10th the cost of relational databases. Amazon Timestream saves you time and cost in managing the lifecycle of time series data by keeping recent data in memory and moving historical data to a cost optimized storage tier based upon user defined policies. Amazon Timestream’s purpose-built query engine lets you access and analyze recent and historical data together, without needing to specify explicitly in the query whether the data resides in the in-memory or cost-optimized tier. Amazon Timestream has built-in time series analytics functions, helping you identify trends and patterns in your data in near real-time. Amazon Timestream is serverless and automatically scales up or down to adjust capacity and performance, so you don’t need to manage the underlying infrastructure, freeing you to focus on building your applications.

cards.title: Record details
cards.helpText:
A record represents a time-series data point being written into Timestream. Each record contains three components : Dimensions, Measures, and a Timestamp.

Dimension: Represents the metadata attributes of the time series. For example, the name and Availability Zone of an EC2 instance or the name of the manufacturer of a wind turbine are dimensions.

Measure name: The name of the metric/event that you want to monitor. For example, the CPU utilization of an EC2 instance.

Measure Value: Represents the data attribute of the time series. For example, the CPU utilization of an EC2 instance or the RPM of a wind turbine are measures.

Timestamp: The timestamp of when the measure was collected and the timestamp unit, which represents the granularity of the timestamp.

components.label: Approx size of each dimension value
Number of bigint, double, timestamp measures

cards.title: Memory store writes
cards.helpText:
The memory store charges are calculated based on the amount of time-series data stored in the memory store of each Amazon Timestream table.

Data batching: Batching multiple records in a single write request helps reduce the cost of writes. See batching time-series events for details. You can batch up to 100 records in a single write request.

Automatic detection of repeated data: When you batch multiple records to Timestream, it will automatically recognize any repeating information in the records (ex: Dimension value such as region repeating across records ) and only charge you once for it. For conservative estimates assume 0% of the data repeated.

cards.title: Magnetic store writes
cards.helpText: The magnetic store charges are calculated based on the amount of time-series data stored in the magnetic store of each Amazon Timestream table.

cards.title: Queries
cards.helpText:
Amazon Timestream for Live Analytics measures the compute capacity allocated to you for your query needs in Timestream compute unit (TCU). When you run queries in Timestream for Live Analytics, the service allocates TCUs on-demand based on the complexity of your queries and the amount of data being processed. Note that, TCU’s can execute multiple queries and the service scales when the existing TCU cannot serve the incoming queries.
Assumptions in the pricing calculator:
1/ The calculator assumes a query latency of 1 second. If your actual query latency is lower (e.g., 200ms) and are spread uniformly across the minute, the same TCU can serve more queries, potentially increasing the query capacity by 5 times.
2/ For real-time queries, the calculator assumes a uniform distribution of queries across the minute. If you have a low query volume (<60 queries per minute) and send queries at a fixed interval (e.g., once a minute), you may only use the compute units for 50% of the estimated time, which could reduce your actual costs by 50%.
Learn more: Timestream developer guide

cards.title: Storage
cards.helpText:
Configure the data retention of the memory store and magnetic store to address your business need. The memory store is optimized for high throughput data ingestion and fast point-in-time queries. For instance, if you need to visualize the granular metric for the last 2 hours, set your memory store retention to 2 hours. The magnetic store is optimized for long term data storage and fast analytical queries. For instance, if you need to store the historical data for 10 years, set the magnetic store retention period to 10 years.

The default value for memory store is 12 hours and magnetic store is 10 years. These default retention settings address majority of the use cases. Kindly adjust the values based on your use case.

components.label: Memory store retention
components.description: Enter the duration you want to store your data in memory store.

components.label: Magnetic store retention
